format_full_name: Executable and Linkable Format — Shared Library (ELF .so)
description: The Linux ELF shared library format. A shared library uses the same ELF format as an executable; the differences lie in the file type (ET_DYN), mandatory position-independent code, the dynamic symbol table and string table, and the absence of a fixed entry point.

variants.name: ELF64 64-bit Shared Library (exports functions, may import from other libraries)
variants.description: 64-bit Linux shared library (.so) with exported symbols and optional imports from other shared libraries. All code must be position-independent using RIP-relative addressing. e_type is always ET_DYN (3) and e_entry is 0 or an unused stub.

required_components.name: ELF Header
required_components.description: Mandatory 64-byte header at the very start of the file. Identical to an executable ELF header except e_type = ET_DYN (3) and e_entry = 0 (or an unused stub address). Identifies the file as a shared library.

fields.name: e_entry
fields.note: 0 — shared libraries are not executed directly; no entry point

fields.name: e_phoff
fields.note: Offset of Program Header Table (immediately after ELF header)

fields.name: e_shoff
fields.note: Offset of Section Header Table; 0 if stripped

fields.name: e_phentsize
fields.note: Size of one Program Header entry = 56

fields.name: e_shentsize
fields.note: Size of one Section Header entry = 64

fields.name: e_shnum
fields.note: Number of Section Header entries; 0 if none

fields.name: e_shstrndx
fields.note: Index of .shstrtab section; SHN_UNDEF(0) if no section headers

code_example.source:
; === ELF64 Header for Shared Library (64 bytes at file offset 0x0000) ===
elf_header:
    db 0x7F, 'E', 'L', 'F'     ; e_ident magic
    db 2                        ; EI_CLASS  = ELFCLASS64
    db 1                        ; EI_DATA   = little-endian
    db 1                        ; EI_VERSION
    db 0                        ; EI_OSABI  = System V
    times 8 db 0                ; EI_ABIVERSION + padding
    dw 3                        ; e_type    = ET_DYN (shared library)
    dw 62                       ; e_machine = EM_X86_64
    dd 1                        ; e_version
    dq 0                        ; e_entry   = 0 (no entry point)
    dq phdr_table               ; e_phoff  (Program Header Table offset)
    dq shdr_table               ; e_shoff  (Section Header Table offset; 0 if stripped)
    dd 0                        ; e_flags
    dw 64                       ; e_ehsize
    dw 56                       ; e_phentsize
    dw PHDR_COUNT               ; e_phnum
    dw 64                       ; e_shentsize
    dw SHDR_COUNT               ; e_shnum
    dw SHSTRNDX                 ; e_shstrndx

required_components.name: PT_DYNAMIC Segment
required_components.description: Points to the .dynamic section, which lists all shared library dependencies (DT_NEEDED), the SONAME (DT_SONAME), and all dynamic linking metadata. Required for every shared library. No PT_INTERP is present — libraries are not executed directly.

fields.name: p_filesz
fields.note: Size of the .dynamic section in the file

code_example.source:
; === PT_DYNAMIC Program Header Entry (56 bytes) ===
phdr_dynamic:
    dd 2                        ; p_type   = PT_DYNAMIC
    dd 6                        ; p_flags  = PF_R | PF_W
    dq dynamic_offset           ; p_offset
    dq dynamic_vaddr            ; p_vaddr
    dq dynamic_vaddr            ; p_paddr
    dq dynamic_size             ; p_filesz
    dq dynamic_size             ; p_memsz
    dq 8                        ; p_align

; === .dynamic Section (array of Elf64_Dyn entries, 16 bytes each) ===
section .dynamic
dynamic:
    dq 14, soname_offset        ; DT_SONAME  'libmylib.so.1'
    dq  1, dynstr_needed_offset ; DT_NEEDED  (if importing from another .so)
    dq  5, dynstr_vaddr         ; DT_STRTAB  -> .dynstr
    dq 10, dynstr_size          ; DT_STRSZ
    dq  6, dynsym_vaddr         ; DT_SYMTAB  -> .dynsym
    dq 11, 24                   ; DT_SYMENT  = 24
    dq 0x6FFFFEF5, gnu_hash_va  ; DT_GNU_HASH -> .gnu.hash
    dq  7, rela_dyn_vaddr       ; DT_RELA    -> .rela.dyn
    dq  8, rela_dyn_size        ; DT_RELASZ
    dq  9, 24                   ; DT_RELAENT = 24
    dq  3, gotplt_vaddr         ; DT_PLTGOT  -> .got.plt
    dq  2, rela_plt_size        ; DT_PLTRELSZ
    dq 20, 7                    ; DT_PLTREL  = DT_RELA
    dq 23, rela_plt_vaddr       ; DT_JMPREL  -> .rela.plt
    dq  0, 0                    ; DT_NULL    (terminator)

required_components.name: Dynamic Symbol Table (.dynsym)
required_components.description: Runtime-visible symbol table containing all exported functions/data and any imported symbols. Exported symbols have st_shndx pointing to a valid section and st_value set to the symbol's virtual address. Imported symbols have st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF (0) and st_value = 0. Entry 0 is always the null symbol.

fields.name: st_shndx
fields.note: Section where symbol is defined; SHN_UNDEF (0) for imports

fields.name: st_value
fields.note: Virtual address of the function/data; 0 for imports

fields.name: st_size
fields.note: Size of the symbol; 0 if unknown

code_example.source:
; === .dynsym Section (24 bytes per Elf64_Sym entry) ===
section .dynsym
dynsym:
    ; Entry 0: null symbol (required)
    dd 0                        ; st_name  = 0 (empty string)
    db 0                        ; st_info  = STB_LOCAL | STT_NOTYPE
    db 0                        ; st_other = STV_DEFAULT
    dw 0                        ; st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF
    dq 0                        ; st_value = 0
    dq 0                        ; st_size  = 0

    ; Entry 1: export 'add_numbers' function
    dd add_numbers_name_offset  ; st_name  (offset in .dynstr)
    db 0x12                     ; st_info  = STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; st_other = STV_DEFAULT
    dw .text_shndx              ; st_shndx = section index of .text
    dq add_numbers              ; st_value = VA of add_numbers
    dq add_numbers_size         ; st_size  = function length

    ; Entry 2: import 'printf' (if importing from libc)
    dd printf_name_offset       ; st_name
    db 0x12                     ; st_info  = STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; st_other = STV_DEFAULT
    dw 0                        ; st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF (imported)
    dq 0                        ; st_value = 0
    dq 0                        ; st_size  = 0

required_components.name: Dynamic String Table (.dynstr)
required_components.description: Null-terminated ASCII strings referenced by .dynsym entries (via st_name) and by .dynamic entries (DT_SONAME, DT_NEEDED). The first byte must be 0x00 (empty string for the null symbol at .dynsym index 0).

fields.name: SONAME string
fields.note: Canonical library name referenced by DT_SONAME

code_example.source:
; === .dynstr Section (null-terminated ASCII strings) ===
section .dynstr
dynstr:
    db 0                        ; offset 0: empty string (null symbol)
soname_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libmylib.so.1', 0       ; DT_SONAME library name
add_numbers_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'add_numbers', 0         ; exported function name
get_value_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'get_value', 0           ; exported function name
libc_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libc.so.6', 0           ; DT_NEEDED library name (if importing)
dynstr_end:
dynstr_size equ dynstr_end - dynstr

required_components.name: GNU Hash Table (.gnu.hash)
required_components.description: Modern GNU hash table for fast dynamic symbol lookup, pointed to by DT_GNU_HASH in .dynamic. Contains a header (nbuckets, symoffset, bloom_size, bloom_shift), a Bloom filter array, a bucket array, and a chain array with one 32-bit hash per symbol.

fields.name: symoffset
fields.note: Index of first symbol in the hash chains (skips local symbols)

fields.name: bloom[]
fields.note: Bloom filter array; enables fast 'definitely not here' checks

fields.name: buckets[]
fields.note: Each bucket is a .dynsym index or 0 if empty

fields.name: chains[]
fields.note: One 32-bit hash per symbol; bit 0 set marks end of chain

code_example.source:
; === .gnu.hash Section (GNU hash table for symbol lookup) ===
; The linker (ld -shared) generates this section automatically.
; For hand-crafted shared libraries, the structure is:
section .gnu.hash
gnu_hash:
    dd NBUCKETS                 ; nbuckets
    dd SYMOFFSET                ; symoffset (first hashed symbol index)
    dd BLOOM_SIZE               ; bloom_size (number of 8-byte bloom words)
    dd BLOOM_SHIFT              ; bloom_shift
    ; bloom filter: BLOOM_SIZE × 8-byte words
    ; buckets: NBUCKETS × 4-byte entries
    ; chains: one 4-byte hash per symbol from symoffset onward

required_components.name: Relocation Table (.rela.dyn)
required_components.description: Dynamic relocation table for non-PLT fixups. Contains R_X86_64_RELATIVE entries for internal pointer adjustments (base + addend), R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT entries for external data symbols, and other relocation types as needed. Processed by the dynamic linker at load time.

fields.name: r_offset
fields.note: Address in the loaded image to apply the relocation

fields.name: r_info
fields.note: Symbol index (high 32 bits) + relocation type (low 32 bits)

code_example.source:
; === .rela.dyn Section (Elf64_Rela, 24 bytes each) ===
section .rela.dyn
rela_dyn:
    ; R_X86_64_RELATIVE: internal pointer fixup (base + addend)
    dq got_my_data_vaddr        ; r_offset: VA of GOT slot to patch
    dq (0 << 32) | 8            ; r_info:   sym 0 | R_X86_64_RELATIVE (8)
    dq my_data_vaddr            ; r_addend: address of my_data

    ; R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT: external data symbol
    dq got_extern_var_vaddr     ; r_offset: VA of GOT slot
    dq (sym_idx << 32) | 6      ; r_info:   symbol index | R_X86_64_GLOB_DAT (6)
    dq 0                        ; r_addend: 0

variants.name: ELF64 64-bit Shared Library (no external dependencies)
variants.description: Self-contained 64-bit shared library with no external imports. Exports functions via .dynsym. No .plt or .got.plt needed. All code uses RIP-relative addressing.

required_components.name: ELF Header
required_components.description: Identical to so64_dynamic ELF header. e_type = ET_DYN (3), e_entry = 0. All code is self-contained; no external symbols are imported.

fields.name: e_shoff
fields.note: Section Header Table offset; 0 if stripped

code_example.source:
; === ELF64 Header for Self-Contained Shared Library ===
elf_header:
    db 0x7F, 'E', 'L', 'F'     ; magic
    db 2                        ; EI_CLASS  = ELFCLASS64
    db 1                        ; EI_DATA   = little-endian
    db 1                        ; EI_VERSION
    db 0                        ; EI_OSABI  = System V
    times 8 db 0                ; padding
    dw 3                        ; e_type    = ET_DYN
    dw 62                       ; e_machine = EM_X86_64
    dd 1                        ; e_version
    dq 0                        ; e_entry   = 0 (no entry point)
    dq phdr_table               ; e_phoff
    dq shdr_table               ; e_shoff
    dd 0                        ; e_flags
    dw 64                       ; e_ehsize
    dw 56                       ; e_phentsize
    dw PHDR_COUNT               ; e_phnum
    dw 64                       ; e_shentsize
    dw SHDR_COUNT               ; e_shnum
    dw SHSTRNDX                 ; e_shstrndx

required_components.name: PT_DYNAMIC Segment
required_components.description: Points to the .dynamic section. For a self-contained library, .dynamic contains DT_SONAME, DT_STRTAB, DT_STRSZ, DT_SYMTAB, DT_SYMENT, DT_GNU_HASH, and optionally DT_RELA/DT_RELASZ/DT_RELAENT for internal pointer fixups. No DT_NEEDED, DT_PLTGOT, or DT_JMPREL entries are present.

code_example.source:
; === PT_DYNAMIC for self-contained .so (no DT_NEEDED) ===
phdr_dynamic:
    dd 2                        ; p_type   = PT_DYNAMIC
    dd 6                        ; p_flags  = PF_R | PF_W
    dq dynamic_offset           ; p_offset
    dq dynamic_vaddr            ; p_vaddr
    dq dynamic_vaddr            ; p_paddr
    dq dynamic_size             ; p_filesz
    dq dynamic_size             ; p_memsz
    dq 8                        ; p_align

section .dynamic
dynamic:
    dq 14, soname_offset        ; DT_SONAME  'libmylib.so.1'
    dq  5, dynstr_vaddr         ; DT_STRTAB  -> .dynstr
    dq 10, dynstr_size          ; DT_STRSZ
    dq  6, dynsym_vaddr         ; DT_SYMTAB  -> .dynsym
    dq 11, 24                   ; DT_SYMENT  = 24
    dq 0x6FFFFEF5, gnu_hash_va  ; DT_GNU_HASH -> .gnu.hash
    dq  0, 0                    ; DT_NULL    (terminator)

required_components.name: Dynamic Symbol Table (.dynsym)
required_components.description: Contains only exported symbols (no SHN_UNDEF imports). All st_shndx values point to valid sections and all st_value fields hold the symbol's VA. Entry 0 is the null symbol.

fields.name: st_shndx
fields.note: Section where symbol is defined (never SHN_UNDEF)

fields.name: st_size
fields.note: Size of the symbol in bytes

code_example.source:
; === .dynsym for self-contained library (exports only) ===
section .dynsym
dynsym:
    ; Entry 0: null symbol
    dd 0
    db 0
    db 0
    dw 0
    dq 0
    dq 0

    ; Entry 1: export 'add_numbers'
    dd add_numbers_name_offset
    db 0x12                     ; STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; STV_DEFAULT
    dw .text_shndx
    dq add_numbers
    dq add_numbers_end - add_numbers

    ; Entry 2: export 'get_value'
    dd get_value_name_offset
    db 0x12                     ; STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; STV_DEFAULT
    dw .text_shndx
    dq get_value
    dq get_value_end - get_value

required_components.name: Dynamic String Table (.dynstr)
required_components.description: Null-terminated strings for all exported symbol names and the SONAME. No DT_NEEDED library names are present since the library has no external dependencies.

code_example.source:
; === .dynstr for self-contained library ===
section .dynstr
dynstr:
    db 0                        ; offset 0: empty string
soname_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libmylib.so.1', 0       ; SONAME
add_numbers_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'add_numbers', 0
get_value_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'get_value', 0
dynstr_end:
dynstr_size equ dynstr_end - dynstr

required_components.name: GNU Hash Table (.gnu.hash)
required_components.description: GNU hash table for fast symbol lookup by the dynamic linker. Same structure as so64_dynamic: header, Bloom filter, buckets, and chains. Automatically generated by ld -shared.

fields.name: chains[]
fields.note: Per-symbol hash values; bit 0 marks end of chain

code_example.source:
; === .gnu.hash (generated by linker) ===
; For a self-contained library with N exported symbols:
section .gnu.hash
gnu_hash:
    dd NBUCKETS
    dd SYMOFFSET
    dd BLOOM_SIZE
    dd BLOOM_SHIFT
    ; bloom filter words, buckets, and chains follow

variants.name: ELF32 32-bit Shared Library (exports functions, may import)
variants.description: 32-bit Linux shared library. Uses GOT-pointer thunk for position-independent code since 32-bit x86 lacks RIP-relative addressing. All code must use __x86.get_pc_thunk and GOT-relative addressing.

required_components.name: ELF Header (32-bit)
required_components.description: 52-byte ELF32 header. EI_CLASS = 1 (ELFCLASS32), e_machine = 3 (EM_386), e_type = ET_DYN (3), e_entry = 0. Addresses and offsets are 4 bytes. Program Header entries are 32 bytes each.

fields.name: e_entry
fields.note: 0 — no entry point for shared library

fields.name: e_phoff
fields.note: Program Header Table at offset 52 (immediately after header)

code_example.source:
; === ELF32 Header for 32-bit Shared Library (52 bytes) ===
elf_header:
    db 0x7F, 'E', 'L', 'F'     ; magic
    db 1                        ; EI_CLASS  = ELFCLASS32
    db 1                        ; EI_DATA   = little-endian
    db 1                        ; EI_VERSION
    db 0                        ; EI_OSABI
    times 8 db 0                ; padding
    dw 3                        ; e_type    = ET_DYN (shared library)
    dw 3                        ; e_machine = EM_386
    dd 1                        ; e_version
    dd 0                        ; e_entry   = 0 (no entry point)
    dd phdr_table               ; e_phoff
    dd shdr_table               ; e_shoff
    dd 0                        ; e_flags
    dw 52                       ; e_ehsize
    dw 32                       ; e_phentsize
    dw PHDR_COUNT               ; e_phnum
    dw 40                       ; e_shentsize
    dw SHDR_COUNT               ; e_shnum
    dw SHSTRNDX                 ; e_shstrndx

required_components.name: PT_DYNAMIC Segment
required_components.description: 32-bit PT_DYNAMIC entry (32 bytes). Points to the .dynamic section. Each .dynamic entry is 8 bytes (Elf32_Dyn: 4-byte d_tag + 4-byte d_val). DT_SYMENT = 16 (size of Elf32_Sym).

fields.name: p_flags
fields.note: PF_R | PF_W (NOTE: p_flags at offset 0x18 in ELF32)

code_example.source:
; === ELF32 PT_DYNAMIC Program Header Entry (32 bytes) ===
phdr_dynamic:
    dd 2                        ; p_type   = PT_DYNAMIC
    dd dynamic_offset           ; p_offset
    dd dynamic_vaddr            ; p_vaddr
    dd dynamic_vaddr            ; p_paddr
    dd dynamic_size             ; p_filesz
    dd dynamic_size             ; p_memsz
    dd 6                        ; p_flags  = PF_R | PF_W
    dd 4                        ; p_align

; === .dynamic Section (Elf32_Dyn, 8 bytes each) ===
section .dynamic
dynamic:
    dd 14, soname_offset        ; DT_SONAME
    dd  1, dynstr_needed_offset ; DT_NEEDED
    dd  5, dynstr_vaddr         ; DT_STRTAB
    dd 10, dynstr_size          ; DT_STRSZ
    dd  6, dynsym_vaddr         ; DT_SYMTAB
    dd 11, 16                   ; DT_SYMENT = 16 (Elf32_Sym)
    dd 0x6FFFFEF5, gnu_hash_va  ; DT_GNU_HASH
    dd 17, rel_dyn_vaddr        ; DT_REL    -> .rel.dyn
    dd 18, rel_dyn_size         ; DT_RELSZ
    dd 19, 8                    ; DT_RELENT = 8
    dd  3, got_vaddr            ; DT_PLTGOT
    dd  2, rel_plt_size         ; DT_PLTRELSZ
    dd 20, 17                   ; DT_PLTREL = DT_REL
    dd 23, rel_plt_vaddr        ; DT_JMPREL
    dd  0, 0                    ; DT_NULL

required_components.name: Dynamic Symbol Table (.dynsym, 16-byte entries)
required_components.description: 32-bit dynamic symbol table. Each Elf32_Sym entry is 16 bytes. The field order differs from ELF64: st_name, st_value, st_size come first, followed by st_info, st_other, st_shndx.

fields.name: st_size
fields.note: Size of the symbol in bytes

code_example.source:
; === .dynsym Section (16 bytes per Elf32_Sym entry) ===
section .dynsym
dynsym:
    ; Entry 0: null symbol
    dd 0                        ; st_name
    dd 0                        ; st_value
    dd 0                        ; st_size
    db 0                        ; st_info
    db 0                        ; st_other
    dw 0                        ; st_shndx

    ; Entry 1: export 'add_numbers'
    dd add_numbers_name_offset  ; st_name
    dd add_numbers              ; st_value (32-bit VA)
    dd add_numbers_size         ; st_size
    db 0x12                     ; st_info  = STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; st_other = STV_DEFAULT
    dw .text_shndx              ; st_shndx

    ; Entry 2: import 'printf' from libc
    dd printf_name_offset       ; st_name
    dd 0                        ; st_value = 0 (undefined)
    dd 0                        ; st_size  = 0
    db 0x12                     ; st_info  = STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0                        ; st_other
    dw 0                        ; st_shndx = SHN_UNDEF

required_components.name: Dynamic String Table (.dynstr)
required_components.description: Same layout as the 64-bit .dynstr: null-terminated ASCII strings for all symbol names, the SONAME, and DT_NEEDED library names. First byte is 0x00.

code_example.source:
; === .dynstr Section (32-bit shared library) ===
section .dynstr
dynstr:
    db 0                        ; offset 0: empty string
soname_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libmylib.so.1', 0       ; SONAME
add_numbers_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'add_numbers', 0
get_value_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'get_value', 0
libc_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libc.so.6', 0           ; DT_NEEDED
dynstr_end:
dynstr_size equ dynstr_end - dynstr

required_components.name: Relocation Table (.rel.dyn, no addend)
required_components.description: 32-bit relocation table using Elf32_Rel (no addend field). Contains R_386_RELATIVE entries for internal pointer fixups, R_386_GLOB_DAT for external data, and R_386_JMP_SLOT for PLT GOT slots (in .rel.plt). Each entry is 8 bytes: 4-byte r_offset + 4-byte r_info.

fields.name: r_offset
fields.note: Address in the loaded image to apply the relocation

fields.name: r_info
fields.note: Symbol index (high 24 bits) + relocation type (low 8 bits)

code_example.source:
; === .rel.dyn Section (Elf32_Rel, 8 bytes each, no addend) ===
section .rel.dyn
rel_dyn:
    ; R_386_RELATIVE: internal pointer fixup
    dd got_my_data_vaddr        ; r_offset: VA of GOT slot to patch
    dd (0 << 8) | 8             ; r_info:   sym 0 | R_386_RELATIVE (8)

    ; R_386_GLOB_DAT: external data symbol
    dd got_extern_vaddr         ; r_offset
    dd (sym_idx << 8) | 6       ; r_info:   symbol index | R_386_GLOB_DAT (6)

variants.name: ELF32 32-bit Shared Library (no external dependencies)
variants.description: Self-contained 32-bit shared library. No external imports. Uses GOT-pointer thunk for PIC. No .plt needed.

required_components.name: ELF Header (32-bit)
required_components.description: 52-byte ELF32 header. Same as so32_dynamic: EI_CLASS = 1, e_machine = EM_386 (3), e_type = ET_DYN (3), e_entry = 0. Self-contained — no imported symbols.

code_example.source:
; === ELF32 Header for Self-Contained 32-bit Shared Library ===
elf_header:
    db 0x7F, 'E', 'L', 'F'     ; magic
    db 1                        ; EI_CLASS  = ELFCLASS32
    db 1                        ; EI_DATA   = little-endian
    db 1                        ; EI_VERSION
    db 0                        ; EI_OSABI
    times 8 db 0                ; padding
    dw 3                        ; e_type    = ET_DYN
    dw 3                        ; e_machine = EM_386
    dd 1                        ; e_version
    dd 0                        ; e_entry   = 0
    dd phdr_table               ; e_phoff
    dd shdr_table               ; e_shoff
    dd 0                        ; e_flags
    dw 52                       ; e_ehsize
    dw 32                       ; e_phentsize
    dw PHDR_COUNT               ; e_phnum
    dw 40                       ; e_shentsize
    dw SHDR_COUNT               ; e_shnum
    dw SHSTRNDX                 ; e_shstrndx

required_components.name: PT_DYNAMIC Segment
required_components.description: 32-bit PT_DYNAMIC entry pointing to .dynamic. No DT_NEEDED entries since the library has no external dependencies. No DT_PLTGOT or DT_JMPREL since there is no PLT.

code_example.source:
; === ELF32 PT_DYNAMIC for self-contained .so ===
phdr_dynamic:
    dd 2                        ; p_type   = PT_DYNAMIC
    dd dynamic_offset           ; p_offset
    dd dynamic_vaddr            ; p_vaddr
    dd dynamic_vaddr            ; p_paddr
    dd dynamic_size             ; p_filesz
    dd dynamic_size             ; p_memsz
    dd 6                        ; p_flags  = PF_R | PF_W
    dd 4                        ; p_align

section .dynamic
dynamic:
    dd 14, soname_offset        ; DT_SONAME
    dd  5, dynstr_vaddr         ; DT_STRTAB
    dd 10, dynstr_size          ; DT_STRSZ
    dd  6, dynsym_vaddr         ; DT_SYMTAB
    dd 11, 16                   ; DT_SYMENT = 16 (Elf32_Sym)
    dd 0x6FFFFEF5, gnu_hash_va  ; DT_GNU_HASH
    dd  0, 0                    ; DT_NULL

required_components.name: Dynamic Symbol Table (.dynsym)
required_components.description: 32-bit dynamic symbol table with only exported symbols (no SHN_UNDEF imports). Each entry is 16 bytes with the ELF32 field order: st_name, st_value, st_size, st_info, st_other, st_shndx.

code_example.source:
; === .dynsym for 32-bit self-contained library ===
section .dynsym
dynsym:
    ; Entry 0: null symbol
    dd 0, 0, 0
    db 0, 0
    dw 0

    ; Entry 1: export 'add_numbers'
    dd add_numbers_name_offset
    dd add_numbers
    dd add_numbers_size
    db 0x12                     ; STB_GLOBAL | STT_FUNC
    db 0
    dw .text_shndx

    ; Entry 2: export 'get_value'
    dd get_value_name_offset
    dd get_value
    dd get_value_size
    db 0x12
    db 0
    dw .text_shndx

required_components.name: Dynamic String Table (.dynstr)
required_components.description: Null-terminated strings for exported symbol names and the SONAME. No DT_NEEDED library names since the library has no external dependencies.

code_example.source:
; === .dynstr for 32-bit self-contained library ===
section .dynstr
dynstr:
    db 0                        ; empty string
soname_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'libmylib.so.1', 0
add_numbers_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'add_numbers', 0
get_value_name_offset equ $ - dynstr
    db 'get_value', 0
dynstr_end:
dynstr_size equ dynstr_end - dynstr
